idea-plugin.name: Karato - Agent for Sakana AI fugu
idea-plugin.description:
Karato is an in-IDE agent for Sakana AI Fugu (and fugu-ultra). It drives the OpenAI Codex CLI — configured with Sakana's Fugu provider — as a subprocess and renders its streamed events as a multi-tab chat tool window: chat with the agent, watch it run commands and edit files in your project, approve actions inline, and review changes as they happen.

Features

  • Multi-tab chat with inline tool cards (run command / web search / file edits).
  • Interactive approvals and structured prompts shown inline.
  • GUI-only setup — installs Codex, writes the provider config, stores your API key.
  • Reads your existing Claude/Codex project files (CLAUDE.md, .claude/, project memory) so you don't paste them each turn.
  • Mirrors your Claude MCP servers (e.g. Playwright) into Codex so they work here too.

Data & external services

Karato is a client for your own accounts and tools. To function it: sends your prompts and selected project context (the files listed above) to the Sakana AI API via the Codex CLI; runs the Codex CLI and, when MCP is enabled, MCP servers (e.g. via npx) as subprocesses; reads the listed Claude/Codex configuration files; and writes MCP entries to ~/.codex/config.toml. Your Sakana API key is stored in the IDE's PasswordSafe and passed to Codex via an environment variable. Karato collects no telemetry and sends nothing to the author. A Sakana account/API key and network access are required.

Open source (Apache-2.0) — github.com/Ardelhite/idea-ai-integration.
